I think people underestimate how much a warm drink can help.
Some customers smile before they even sit down.
Others take a little longer.
The coffee tastes better when the rain starts.
I like quiet nights.
Not because they are peaceful.
Because they make the small things easier to notice.
Someone left a note on a napkin earlier.
I did not read it.
Some thoughts deserve privacy.
I recognize more faces than names these days.
That feels normal now.
The windows always look beautiful after it rains.
The city feels softer at night.
I wonder if everyone sees it that way.
A customer thanked me for remembering their order.
I remembered because they always hesitate before choosing.
People have little habits they do not notice.
I notice them.
The flowers on the tables are starting to wilt.
I should replace them tomorrow.
There is a comfort in familiar routines.
Even small ones.
I think everyone needs a place they can return to.
Somewhere that feels unchanged.
The coffee machine sounds different when the room is empty.
The night shift always feels longer when it is quiet.
Not in a bad way.
Just different.
I watched someone stare out the window for nearly an hour.
Sometimes thinking is enough.
The city lights look like stars from a distance.
Maybe that is why I like watching them.
A customer fell asleep at a table once.
We let them rest.
Everyone looks a little tired these days.
Some people hide it better than others.
I like hearing people laugh.
It changes the whole room.
The rain always brings more stories through the door.
Some stay longer than others.
There is a certain peace that only exists after midnight.
It never lasts long.
But it is nice while it does.
I have always liked listening more than talking.
People reveal a lot without realizing it.
The city feels less rushed at this hour.
Like it finally has time to breathe.
Someone complimented the coffee tonight.
I did not have the heart to tell them Vincent made it.
The lights outside are reflecting off the pavement again.
Everything looks brighter after rain.
I think familiar places become special over time.
Not all at once.
Little by little.
The night is getting late.
Still, nobody seems ready to leave.
I understand that feeling.
Some nights are worth staying awake for.
The District always feels a little more alive after dark.
